docs.title: suitesparse-graphblas
docs.text: Low-level Python CFFI bindings for SuiteSparse:GraphBLAS.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: suitesparse-graphblas exposes the raw ffi and lib symbols from the SuiteSparse:GraphBLAS C library and provides a functional API in the suitesparse_graphblas.api subpackage. The functional API consists of module-level functions operating on opaque CFFI handles for each GraphBLAS type.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
from suitesparse_graphblas import initialize, lib
from suitesparse_graphblas.api import matrix

initialize()

# Create a 3x3 boolean matrix
A = matrix.matrix_new(lib.GrB_BOOL, 3, 3)
matrix.set_bool(A, True, 0, 1)
matrix.set_bool(A, True, 1, 2)

print(matrix.matrix_nvals(A))  # 2
print(matrix.matrix_shape(A))  # (3, 3)

docs.title: API Modules
docs.text:
The functional API covers all GraphBLAS types:

Module Type Description matrix GrB_Matrix Sparse matrix operations vector GrB_Vector Sparse vector operations scalar GxB_Scalar Scalar values iterator GxB_Iterator Matrix/vector iteration context GxB_Context Thread configuration grb_type GrB_Type Type definitions unaryop GrB_UnaryOp Unary operators binaryop GrB_BinaryOp Binary operators indexunaryop GrB_IndexUnaryOp Index unary operators indexbinaryop GxB_IndexBinaryOp Index binary operators monoid GrB_Monoid Monoids semiring GrB_Semiring Semirings descriptor GrB_Descriptor Operation descriptors selectop GxB_SelectOp Select operators (deprecated) container GxB_Container Containers global_options — Global option get/set (GxB) global_ GrB_Global Global get/set (GrB 2.1) io — Serialization and binary I/O

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install suitesparse-graphblas

Or from source with a local GraphBLAS build:

export GraphBLAS_ROOT="/path/to/graphblas"
pip install -e . --no-deps

docs.title: binaryop_free(op)
docs.text:
Free a binary operator.

op = ffi.new("GrB_BinaryOp*", lib.GrB_PLUS_INT64) binaryop_free(op)

docs.title: binaryop_new(function, ztype, xtype, ytype, *, free=binaryop_free)
docs.text:
Create a new GrB_BinaryOp from a C function pointer.

function must be a CFFI callback compatible with GxB_binary_function.

The free argument is called when the object is garbage collected, the default is binaryop.binaryop_free(). If free is None then there is no automatic garbage collection and it is up to the user to free the operator.

docs.title: binaryop_new_named(function, ztype, xtype, ytype, name, defn, *, free=binaryop_free)
docs.text:
Create a new named GrB_BinaryOp (GxB extension).

function must be a CFFI callback compatible with GxB_binary_function. name and defn are C strings describing the operator for JIT compilation.

The free argument is called when the object is garbage collected, the default is binaryop.binaryop_free(). If free is None then there is no automatic garbage collection and it is up to the user to free the operator.

docs.title: binaryop_new_indexop(idxbinop, theta, *, free=binaryop_free)
docs.text:
Create a new GrB_BinaryOp from a GxB_IndexBinaryOp and a scalar theta.

The free argument is called when the object is garbage collected, the default is binaryop.binaryop_free(). If free is None then there is no automatic garbage collection and it is up to the user to free the operator.

docs.title: binaryop_wait(op, waitmode=lib.GrB_COMPLETE)
docs.text:
Wait for a binary operator to complete pending operations.

op = ffi.new("GrB_BinaryOp*", lib.GrB_PLUS_INT64) binaryop_wait(op)

docs.title: binaryop_print(op, name='', level=lib.GxB_COMPLETE)
docs.text:
Print a binary operator to stdout.

level controls verbosity: lib.GxB_SILENT, lib.GxB_SUMMARY, lib.GxB_SHORT, lib.GxB_COMPLETE, lib.GxB_SHORT_VERBOSE, or lib.GxB_COMPLETE_VERBOSE.

op = ffi.new("GrB_BinaryOp*", lib.GrB_PLUS_INT64) out = _capture_c_output(binaryop_print, op, 'op', lib.GxB_SHORT) 'plus' in out.lower() True

docs.title: binaryop_fprint(op, f, name='', level=lib.GxB_COMPLETE)
docs.text:
Print a binary operator to a C FILE* stream.

Pass ffi.NULL for f to print to stdout. level controls verbosity (see :func:binaryop_print).

op = ffi.new("GrB_BinaryOp*", lib.GrB_PLUS_INT64) out = _capture_c_output(binaryop_fprint, op, ffi.NULL, 'op', lib.GxB_SHORT) 'plus' in out.lower() True

docs.title: context_engage(ctx)
docs.text:
Engage a context for the calling thread.

An engaged context controls settings like nthreads and chunk for all GraphBLAS operations executed by the calling thread until the context is disengaged.

ctx = context_new() context_engage(ctx) context_disengage(ctx)

docs.title: context_disengage(ctx)
docs.text:
Disengage a context from the calling thread.

After disengaging, the calling thread reverts to the default (world) context settings.

ctx = context_new() context_engage(ctx) context_disengage(ctx)

docs.title: indexbinaryop
docs.text:
GxB_IndexBinaryOp functional API.

There are no built-in GxB_IndexBinaryOp constants in the library, so most functions in this module cannot have doctests without constructing an operator from a CFFI callback. Only indexbinaryop_new creates instances, and it requires a C function pointer.

docs.title: get_serialize_desc(compression=lib.GxB_COMPRESSION_DEFAULT, level=None, nthreads=None)
docs.text:
Create a descriptor for serializing or deserializing.

This returns None (for NULL descriptor) or a pointer to a GrB_Descriptor.

docs.title: serialize_matrix(A, compression=lib.GxB_COMPRESSION_DEFAULT, level=None, *, nthreads=None)
docs.text:
Serialize a Matrix into an array of bytes.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default compression int

One of None, GxB_COMPRESSION_NONE, GxB_COMPRESSION_DEFAULT, GxB_COMPRESSION_LZ4, GxB_COMPRESSION_LZ4HC, or GxB_COMPRESSION_ZSTD

GxB_COMPRESSION_DEFAULT level int

For GxB_COMPRESSION_LZ4HC, should be between 1 and 9, where 9 is most compressed. For GxB_COMPRESSION_ZSTD, should be between 1 and 19, where 19 is most compressed.

None nthreads int

The maximum number of OpenMP threads to use.

None

GxB_COMPRESSION_DEFAULT level int

For GxB_COMPRESSION_LZ4HC, should be between 1 and 9, where 9 is most compressed. For GxB_COMPRESSION_ZSTD, should be between 1 and 19, where 19 is most compressed.

docs.title: matrix_iterator_attach(it, A, desc=None)

Returns lib.GrB_SUCCESS or lib.GxB_EXHAUSTED (if the matrix has no entries). Raises on other errors.

docs.text: Free a matrix.

docs.title: matrix_new(T, nrows=lib.GxB_INDEX_MAX, ncols=lib.GxB_INDEX_MAX, *, free=matrix_free)
docs.text:
Create a new GrB_Matrix of type T and initialize it. The following example creates an eight bit unsigned 2x2 matrix:

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_UINT8, 2, 2) matrix_shape(A) (2, 2)

The default value for nrows and ncols is lib.GxB_INDEX_MAX which creates a Matrix with maximal bounds:

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_UINT8) matrix_shape(A) == (lib.GxB_INDEX_MAX, lib.GxB_INDEX_MAX) True

The free argument is called when the object is garbage collected, the default is matrix.matrix_free(). If free is None then there is no automatic garbage collection and it is up to the user to free the matrix.

docs.title: matrix_type(A)
docs.text:
Return the GraphBLAS type of the vector.

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_UINT8) matrix_type(A) == lib.GrB_UINT8 True

docs.title: matrix_nrows(A)
docs.text:
Return the number of rows in the matrix.

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_UINT8, 2, 3) matrix_nrows(A) 2

docs.title: matrix_ncols(A)
docs.text:
Return the number of columns in the matrix.

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_UINT8, 2, 3) matrix_ncols(A) 3

docs.title: matrix_nvals(A)
docs.text:
Return the number of stored elements in the matrix.

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_UINT8, 2, 3) matrix_nvals(A) 0

docs.title: matrix_shape(A)
docs.text:
Return the shape of the matrix as a two tuple (nrows, ncols)

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_UINT8, 2, 2) matrix_shape(A) (2, 2)

docs.title: matrix_format(A)
docs.text:
Return the format of the matrix.

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_UINT8, 2, 2) matrix_format(A) == lib.GxB_BY_ROW True

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_UINT8, 2, 2) matrix_set_format(A, lib.GxB_BY_COL) matrix_format(A) == lib.GxB_BY_COL True

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 42, 0, 0) matrix_nvals(A) == 1 True matrix_clear(A) matrix_nvals(A) == 0 True

docs.title: matrix_wait(A, waitmode=lib.GrB_COMPLETE)
docs.text:
Wait for a matrix to complete pending operations.

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) matrix_wait(A)

docs.title: matrix_print(A, name='', level=lib.GxB_COMPLETE)
docs.text:
Print a matrix to stdout.

level controls verbosity: lib.GxB_SILENT, lib.GxB_SUMMARY, lib.GxB_SHORT, lib.GxB_COMPLETE, lib.GxB_SHORT_VERBOSE, or lib.GxB_COMPLETE_VERBOSE.

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 42, 0, 0) matrix_wait(A, lib.GrB_MATERIALIZE) out = _capture_c_output(matrix_print, A, 'A', lib.GxB_SHORT) '2x2 GraphBLAS int64_t matrix' in out True '(0,0) 42' in out True

docs.title: matrix_fprint(A, f, name='', level=lib.GxB_COMPLETE)
docs.text:
Print a matrix to a C FILE* stream.

Pass ffi.NULL for f to print to stdout. level controls verbosity (see :func:matrix_print).

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 7, 1, 1) matrix_wait(A, lib.GrB_MATERIALIZE) out = _capture_c_output(matrix_fprint, A, ffi.NULL, 'A', lib.GxB_SHORT) '2x2 GraphBLAS int64_t matrix' in out True '(1,1) 7' in out True

docs.title: matrix_mxm(C, semiring, A, B, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Multiply two matrices using a semiring: C = A (+.*) B.

C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) B = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 2, 0, 0) set_int64(B, 3, 0, 0) matrix_mxm(C, lib.GrB_PLUS_TIMES_SEMIRING_INT64, A, B) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 6 True

docs.title: matrix_ewise_add(C, op, A, B, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Element-wise addition of two matrices using a binary operator.

C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) B = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 2, 0, 0) set_int64(B, 3, 0, 0) matrix_ewise_add(C, lib.GrB_PLUS_INT64, A, B) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 5 True

docs.title: matrix_ewise_mult(C, op, A, B, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Element-wise multiplication of two matrices using a binary operator.

C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) B = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 2, 0, 0) set_int64(B, 3, 0, 0) matrix_ewise_mult(C, lib.GrB_TIMES_INT64, A, B) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 6 True

docs.title: matrix_ewise_union(C, op, A, alpha, B, beta, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Element-wise union of two matrices using a binary operator.

Unlike eWiseAdd, entries present in only one operand use the corresponding default scalar (alpha for A, beta for B).

from suitesparse_graphblas import scalar C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) B = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 2, 0, 0) set_int64(B, 3, 1, 1) a = scalar.scalar_new(lib.GrB_INT64) b = scalar.scalar_new(lib.GrB_INT64) scalar.set_int64(a, 0) scalar.set_int64(b, 0) matrix_ewise_union(C, lib.GrB_PLUS_INT64, A, a, B, b) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 2 True get_int64(C, 1, 1) == 3 True

docs.title: matrix_apply(C, op, A, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Apply a unary operator to each entry of a matrix.

C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, -5, 0, 0) matrix_apply(C, lib.GrB_ABS_INT64, A) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 5 True

docs.title: matrix_apply_first(C, op, x, A, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Apply a binary operator with a scalar bound to the first argument: C = op(x, A).

from suitesparse_graphblas import scalar C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 3, 0, 0) s = scalar.scalar_new(lib.GrB_INT64) scalar.set_int64(s, 10) matrix_apply_first(C, lib.GrB_TIMES_INT64, s, A) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 30 True

docs.title: matrix_apply_second(C, op, A, y, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Apply a binary operator with a scalar bound to the second argument: C = op(A, y).

from suitesparse_graphblas import scalar C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 10, 0, 0) s = scalar.scalar_new(lib.GrB_INT64) scalar.set_int64(s, 3) matrix_apply_second(C, lib.GrB_TIMES_INT64, A, s) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 30 True

docs.title: matrix_select(C, op, A, thunk, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Select entries from a matrix using an index unary operator and scalar thunk.

from suitesparse_graphblas import scalar C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 5, 0, 0) set_int64(A, 1, 1, 1) s = scalar.scalar_new(lib.GrB_INT64) scalar.set_int64(s, 3) matrix_select(C, lib.GrB_VALUEGT_INT64, A, s) matrix_nvals(C) == 1 True get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 5 True

docs.title: matrix_reduce(w, monoid, A, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Reduce a matrix to a vector using a monoid (row-wise reduction).

from suitesparse_graphblas import vector A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 2, 0, 0) set_int64(A, 3, 0, 1) w = vector.vector_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2) matrix_reduce(w, lib.GrB_PLUS_MONOID_INT64, A) vector.get_int64(w, 0) == 5 True

docs.title: matrix_reduce_scalar(s, monoid, A, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Reduce a matrix to a scalar using a monoid.

from suitesparse_graphblas import scalar A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 2, 0, 0) set_int64(A, 3, 1, 1) s = scalar.scalar_new(lib.GrB_INT64) matrix_reduce_scalar(s, lib.GrB_PLUS_MONOID_INT64, A) scalar.get_int64(s) == 5 True

docs.title: matrix_transpose(C, A, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Transpose a matrix.

C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 3, 2) A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 3) set_int64(A, 7, 0, 2) matrix_transpose(C, A) get_int64(C, 2, 0) == 7 True

docs.title: matrix_kronecker(C, op, A, B, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Kronecker product of two matrices using a binary operator.

C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 4, 4) A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) B = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 2, 0, 0) set_int64(B, 3, 0, 0) matrix_kronecker(C, lib.GrB_TIMES_INT64, A, B) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 6 True

docs.title: matrix_diag(C, v, k=0, desc=None)
docs.text:
Build a diagonal matrix from a vector.

The vector v is placed on the k-th diagonal of C (k=0 is the main diagonal, positive is above, negative below).

from suitesparse_graphblas import vector C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 3, 3) v = vector.vector_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 3) vector.set_int64(v, 1, 0) vector.set_int64(v, 2, 1) vector.set_int64(v, 3, 2) matrix_diag(C, v, 0) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 1 True get_int64(C, 1, 1) == 2 True get_int64(C, 2, 2) == 3 True

docs.title: matrix_concat(C, tiles, m, n, desc=None)
docs.text:
Concatenate an m-by-n grid of tile matrices into C.

tiles is a flat list of matrix handles in row-major order, representing an m-by-n grid of submatrices.

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 1, 2) B = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 1, 2) set_int64(A, 1, 0, 0) set_int64(A, 2, 0, 1) set_int64(B, 3, 0, 0) set_int64(B, 4, 0, 1) C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) matrix_concat(C, [A, B], 2, 1) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 1 True get_int64(C, 1, 0) == 3 True

docs.title: matrix_assign(C, A, rows, nrows, cols, ncols, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Assign a submatrix: C[rows, cols] = A.

C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 3, 3) A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 2, 2) set_int64(A, 10, 0, 0) set_int64(A, 20, 1, 1) rows = ffi.new("GrB_Index[2]", [0, 1]) cols = ffi.new("GrB_Index[2]", [0, 1]) matrix_assign(C, A, rows, 2, cols, 2) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 10 True get_int64(C, 1, 1) == 20 True

docs.title: matrix_assign_scalar(C, x, rows, nrows, cols, ncols, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Assign a scalar to a submatrix: C[rows, cols] = x.

from suitesparse_graphblas import scalar C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 3, 3) s = scalar.scalar_new(lib.GrB_INT64) scalar.set_int64(s, 99) rows = ffi.new("GrB_Index[2]", [0, 1]) cols = ffi.new("GrB_Index[2]", [0, 1]) matrix_assign_scalar(C, s, rows, 2, cols, 2) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 99 True get_int64(C, 1, 1) == 99 True

docs.title: matrix_extract(C, A, rows, nrows, cols, ncols, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)

A = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 3, 3) set_int64(A, 42, 1, 2) C = matrix_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 1, 1) rows = ffi.new("GrB_Index[1]", [1]) cols = ffi.new("GrB_Index[1]", [2]) matrix_extract(C, A, rows, 1, cols, 1) get_int64(C, 0, 0) == 42 True

docs.title: matrix_col_extract(w, A, rows, nrows, j, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)

docs.title: monoid_new_bool(op, identity, *, free=monoid_free)

docs.title: vector_free(v)
docs.text: Free a vector.

docs.title: vector_new(T, size=lib.GxB_INDEX_MAX, *, free=vector_free)
docs.text:
Create a new GrB_Vector of type T and initialize it.

A = vector_new(lib.GrB_UINT8, 2) vector_size(A) 2

The default size is lib.GxB_INDEX_MAX.

A = vector_new(lib.GrB_UINT8) vector_size(A) == lib.GxB_INDEX_MAX True

The free argument is called when the object is garbage collected, the default is vector.vector_free(). If free is None then there is no automatic garbage collection and it is up to the user to free the vector.

docs.title: vector_ewise_union(w, op, u, alpha, v, beta, mask=None, accum=None, desc=None)
docs.text:
Element-wise union of two vectors using a binary operator.

Unlike eWiseAdd, entries present in only one operand use the corresponding default scalar (alpha for u, beta for v).

from suitesparse_graphblas import scalar w = vector_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 3) u = vector_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 3) v = vector_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 3) set_int64(u, 2, 0) set_int64(v, 3, 2) a = scalar.scalar_new(lib.GrB_INT64) b = scalar.scalar_new(lib.GrB_INT64) scalar.set_int64(a, 0) scalar.set_int64(b, 0) vector_ewise_union(w, lib.GrB_PLUS_INT64, u, a, v, b) get_int64(w, 0) == 2 True get_int64(w, 2) == 3 True

docs.title: vector_diag(v, A, k=0, desc=None)

v = vector_new(lib.GrB_INT64, 3) set_int64(v, 7, 2) get_int64(v, 2) == 7 True

docs.title: vector_build_fc32(w, indices, vals, nvals, dup)
docs.text:
Build a vector from index and fc32 value arrays.

w = vector_new(lib.GxB_FC32, 3) idx = np.array([0], dtype=np.uint64) vals = np.array([2+3j], dtype=np.complex64) vector_build_fc32(w, idx, vals, 1, lib.GxB_PLUS_FC32) get_fc32(w, 0) == 2+3j True

docs.title: vector_extract_tuples_fc32(v)
docs.text:
Extract all tuples from a vector as numpy arrays.

Returns (indices, vals) as numpy arrays.

v = vector_new(lib.GxB_FC32, 3) set_fc32(v, 2+3j, 0) idx, vals = vector_extract_tuples_fc32(v) len(vals) == 1 True complex(vals[0]) == 2+3j True
